nodes.summary: Merges per-station mention timelines into a single date-keyed series by taking the max value across stations for each date.

nodes.name: extract_points
nodes.summary: Extracts the time-series data points for a given station from a raw GDELT timeline JSON structure.

nodes.name: _ws_headers
nodes.summary: Builds the signed authentication headers required for the Kalshi WebSocket handshake using the Kalshi private-key signer.

nodes.name: KalshiWebSocketClient
nodes.summary: Maintains a resilient authenticated Kalshi WebSocket connection, subscribes to orderbook/trade channels, and applies incoming snapshots and deltas to OrderBookState.

nodes.name: WhaleSignal
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a detected Polymarket whale trade entry, including wallet, market, side, size, entry price, and metadata.

nodes.name: PolymarketClient
nodes.summary: Async Polymarket client that fetches markets and trades, fuzzy-matches them to Kalshi markets, detects volume spikes and whale entries, scores wallet profitability, and emits a price-based signal estimate.

nodes.name: bootstrap_whale_targets
nodes.summary: Discovers candidate whale wallets by scanning large trades across markets, scores their profitability, and returns a ranked list of target wallets.

nodes.name: detect_whale_entries
nodes.summary: Filters recent large trades to identify fresh whale entries above size and recency thresholds, returning structured WhaleSignal objects.

nodes.name: match_market_with_score
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-matches a Kalshi market title to a Polymarket question using tokenization, number compatibility, and subject overlap, returning a match confidence score.

nodes.name: _parse_authority_response
nodes.summary: Parses a Grok weather-authority forecast reply into an AuthorityForecast, extracting temps, precip, confidence, and quotes from the structured PROB/JSON format.

nodes.name: _parse_profile_response
nodes.summary: Parses a Grok profile-topic scan reply into a ProfileScan, clamping probability and uncertainty and extracting post counts and quotes.

nodes.name: XClient
nodes.summary: Async Grok (x.ai) Agent Tools client exposing live X search, weather-authority forecast search, and profile-topic scans over the /v1/responses endpoint.

nodes.name: BaseXStrategy
nodes.summary: Base X search strategy defining the query-build/run/convert lifecycle and translating Grok results into a normalized SignalEstimate.

nodes.name: to_signal_estimate
nodes.summary: Converts a matched Polymarket market price into a normalized SignalEstimate, applying liquidity-aware confidence and recency weighting.

nodes.name: OrderBookState
nodes.summary: Maintains per-ticker bid/ask books and recent trades from WebSocket updates and computes microstructure metrics like best bid/ask, spread, imbalance, and volume velocity.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, runs the async mentions pipeline against GDELT, and prints the resulting signal estimate as JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs the XAgent for a single market and prints its signal estimate as JSON.

nodes.name: _run
nodes.summary: Async routine that bootstraps Polymarket whale targets above a score threshold and persists the resulting wallet list to disk.

nodes.name: RiskManager
nodes.summary: Vets trade ideas against exposure, time-to-close, and edge thresholds, sizes positions using half-Kelly with loss-streak damping, and estimates Kalshi trading fees.

nodes.name: build_mentions_base_signal
nodes.summary: Builds the core mentions SignalEstimate from GDELT base rates and corpus data, blending counts and confidence into a clamped probability and narrative.

nodes.name: build_hearing_schedule_signal
nodes.summary: Derives a mentions signal from committee-hearing schedule records, fuzzy-matching the committee hint and detecting disrupted or cancelled hearings.

nodes.name: build_x_profile_signal
nodes.summary: Builds a leading-indicator mentions signal from an X profile-topic scan, mapping post counts and probability into a narrated SignalEstimate.

nodes.name: build_mentions_live_signal
nodes.summary: Builds a near-real-time mentions signal from the latest live timeline points, clipping to the most recent observation and producing a SignalEstimate.

nodes.name: build_mentions_signal
nodes.summary: Top-level mentions signal builder that delegates to the base-signal builder and validates required base-rate inputs.

nodes.name: build_x_signal
nodes.summary: Builds a normalized SignalEstimate from a raw X/Grok search payload, mapping sentiment direction, probability, post count, and issued time into the common schema.

nodes.name: settlement_context.py
nodes.summary: Parses operator-supplied settlement-source arguments and renders them into a human-readable prompt block plus structured metadata for signal agents.

nodes.name: parse_settlement_arg
nodes.summary: Parses a raw settlement argument string as JSON into a settlement dict, tolerating empty or malformed input.

nodes.name: _render_sources
nodes.summary: Renders a list of settlement-source dicts into a semicolon-joined display string of names and URLs.

nodes.name: format_settlement_block
nodes.summary: Builds a multi-line settlement context block (station, rule, sources) for injection into agent prompts.

nodes.name: settlement_metadata
nodes.summary: Extracts a normalized settlement metadata dict (station, sources) from a settlement payload for downstream tracking.

nodes.name: weather_agent.py
nodes.summary: Weather signal agent that fuses NOAA forecasts, NWS discussion, Open-Meteo ensembles, observed extremes, and independent authority forecasts into multiple weather probability signals for a Kalshi market.
nodes.languageNotes: Async LLM-backed agent that delegates prompting to BaseAgent and stamps deterministic forecast points onto the parsed estimates.

nodes.name: WeatherAgent
nodes.summary: Orchestrates weather data clients (NOAA, Open-Meteo, X/Grok) and signal builders to produce LLM-parsed plus deterministic ensemble and authority probability estimates for a weather market.

nodes.name: contract_terms.py
nodes.summary: Loads, caches, and fetches per-series Kalshi contract settlement terms from a JSON store, with concurrent retried fetches against the Kalshi API.

nodes.name: load_contract_terms
nodes.summary: Loads the cached contract-terms JSON file into an uppercased-series-keyed dict, returning empty when absent.

nodes.name: get_or_fetch
nodes.summary: Returns a single series' cached contract terms or fetches them from the Kalshi API and persists the result.

nodes.name: get_or_fetch_many
nodes.summary: Concurrently fetches contract terms for multiple series under a semaphore with retry, merging into and persisting the cache.

nodes.name: fivethirtyeight.py
nodes.summary: Async client that downloads FiveThirtyEight polling-average CSV files and parses them into row dicts for election signals.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a truststore-backed SSL context to work behind the corporate TLS proxy.

nodes.name: FiveThirtyEightClient
nodes.summary: Aiohttp-based client that fetches and returns parsed FiveThirtyEight poll CSVs for a given poll type.

nodes.name: noaa.py
nodes.summary: Async NOAA/NWS API client providing gridpoint forecasts, area forecast discussions, station coordinates, and observed daily temperature/precip extremes used to settle weather markets.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles NWS grid resolution, timezone-aware observation windows via ZoneInfo, and unit conversion for wind and precipitation.

nodes.name: NOAAClient
nodes.summary: Aiohttp client wrapping the NWS API for gridpoint forecasts, forecast discussions, station coordinates, and observed daily extremes.

nodes.name: _observation_station_candidates
nodes.summary: Generates ordered NWS observation-station ID candidates from a raw station identifier, handling KXXX-style normalization.

nodes.name: open_meteo.py
nodes.summary: Async Open-Meteo client that retrieves GEFS ensemble member forecasts (high/low temp) for a coordinate and target date.
nodes.languageNotes: Truststore SSL context for corporate proxy; maps Kalshi metrics to Open-Meteo daily field names.

nodes.name: OpenMeteoClient
nodes.summary: Aiohttp client fetching Open-Meteo GEFS ensemble member values for a metric at a given location and date.

nodes.name: polls_parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses election market titles into candidate/state queries and computes grade-weighted recent polling margins from FiveThirtyEight rows.

nodes.name: parse_election_title
nodes.summary: Parses a Kalshi election market title and ticker into a structured query (candidate, state, vote-share threshold, poll type).

nodes.name: recent_margin
nodes.summary: Computes a grade-weighted average polling margin for a candidate (optionally scoped to a state) over the most recent N polls.

nodes.name: weather_authorities.py
nodes.summary: Static registry mapping cities to local independent weather-authority social handles, with helpers to resolve authorities and check independence.

nodes.name: get_authorities
nodes.summary: Resolves a city name (via aliases) to its list of registered independent weather-authority handles.

nodes.name: weather_parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses weather market tickers and titles into structured queries (city, metric, threshold band, target date) and classifies NWS discussion tone.

nodes.name: parse_title
nodes.summary: Parses a weather market ticker and title into a structured query with city, metric, threshold band, comparison, and target date.

nodes.name: _parse_band
nodes.summary: Extracts a numeric threshold band (low/high) from a market title or ticker suffix.

nodes.name: parse_discussion
nodes.summary: Classifies an NWS area forecast discussion's tone (warming/cooling/uncertainty cues) into a coarse summary.

nodes.name: weather_settlement.py
nodes.summary: Resolves the authoritative NWS settlement station for a weather series by classifying operator-supplied settlement-source URLs and falling back to series defaults.

nodes.name: _station_from_source
nodes.summary: Extracts a station identifier from a settlement-source URL by inspecting host, query params, and path segments.

nodes.name: resolve_settlement_station
nodes.summary: Resolves the settlement station for a series from operator-provided sources, falling back to per-series defaults.

nodes.name: polls.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs the polls signal pipeline for a single election market and prints the resulting signal estimate as JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI args, fetches polls, computes the recent margin, builds the polls signal, and prints structured output.

nodes.name: weather.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs the WeatherAgent for a single weather market and prints the resulting signal estimates as JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI args, runs the WeatherAgent with settlement context, and prints the estimates and metadata.

nodes.name: polls.py
nodes.summary: Converts a recent polling margin summary into a SignalEstimate probability using a normal-distribution survival function.

nodes.name: build_polls_signal
nodes.summary: Builds a SignalEstimate from a polling margin summary, mapping the margin to a win probability via a normal survival function.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses scipy.stats.norm.sf to map a polling margin and standard error into a clamped probability.

nodes.name: station_coords.py
nodes.summary: Loads, caches, and resolves per-series NWS settlement-station coordinates, fetching from NOAA when uncached and persisting the result.

nodes.name: resolve_station_coordinates
nodes.summary: Resolves a series' settlement-station coordinates from cache or by fetching from NOAA via the resolved settlement station, persisting on success.

nodes.name: station_label_for_series
nodes.summary: Returns the cached display label for a series' settlement station, if any.

nodes.name: fed.py
nodes.summary: Federal Reserve transcript client: fetches and parses Fed speeches (RSS feeds + HTML) and FOMC press-conference PDFs into speaker-attributed records for the mentions archive.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses truststore-backed aiohttp sessions for corporate-proxy TLS, asyncio.Semaphore-bounded concurrency for full-text enrichment, and pypdf for presser PDF extraction.

nodes.name: parse_speech_html
nodes.summary: Strips a Fed speech HTML page down to clean article body text by isolating the content region and removing scripts, tags, and entities.

nodes.name: parse_speech_feed
nodes.summary: Parses a Fed RSS speech feed into speaker-resolved transcript records, deriving the speaker key and event date for each entry.

nodes.name: parse_presser_text
nodes.summary: Segments raw FOMC press-conference text into speaker turns and isolates the chair's remarks into an attributed transcript record.

nodes.name: _feed_entry_speaker
nodes.summary: Heuristically extracts the speaker name from a feed entry's title or author field using regex and comma-splitting.

nodes.name: FedClient
nodes.summary: Async client that fetches Fed speeches and FOMC presser transcripts over aiohttp, enriching speech records with full body text under bounded concurrency.

nodes.name: govinfo.py
nodes.summary: GovInfo CREC (Congressional Record) client: discovers packages and granules, fetches granule summaries and text, and builds speaker-attributed floor-statement records.
nodes.languageNotes: truststore-backed aiohttp with API-key query params; asyncio.Semaphore-bounded granule fetching.

nodes.name: parse_crec_granule_summary
nodes.summary: Parses a CREC granule summary JSON into a normalized dict with speaker, date, text URL, and granule metadata.

nodes.name: build_crec_record
nodes.summary: Assembles a final speaker-resolved transcript record from a granule summary and its plain-text body for the mentions archive.

nodes.name: parse_crec_granules
nodes.summary: Parses a CREC granules listing JSON into granule stubs and the next-page link for pagination.

nodes.name: GovInfoClient
nodes.summary: Async client for the GovInfo API that walks CREC packages and granules and returns built congressional floor-statement records under bounded concurrency.

nodes.name: mentions_parser.py
nodes.summary: Pure-function parsing core for the mentions signal: normalizes text, extracts the target phrase/speaker from Kalshi market titles and settlement rules, and computes base rates from GDELT points.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependency-free module of pure functions with smart-quote/unicode folding via str.translate; relied on by both the live signal and the archive store.

nodes.name: normalize_for_match
nodes.summary: Folds smart punctuation, lowercases, and collapses non-alphanumeric runs to produce a canonical string for phrase matching.

nodes.name: parse_mention_title
nodes.summary: Extracts the tracked phrase and (optional) speaker from a Kalshi mention-market title using ordered regex patterns and guard heuristics.

nodes.name: extract_phrase_from_settlement
nodes.summary: Derives the canonical mention phrase and speaker from a market's settlement metadata (sub-title, rules, sources) when the title alone is ambiguous.

nodes.name: base_rate_from_points
nodes.summary: Computes a smoothed base-rate probability of a mention occurring from a series of historical GDELT data points.

nodes.name: parse_point_datetime
nodes.summary: Parses a GDELT date-string into a UTC datetime, tolerating variable digit lengths and falling back to a sentinel.

nodes.name: speaker_registry.py
nodes.summary: Canonical registry that resolves a parsed speaker name into a SpeakerProfile (role, GDELT stations, transcript venues, X/YouTube handles) via alias and token matching.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclass profile plus module-level alias/entry tables; resolve_speaker falls back to a generic profile for unknown speakers.

nodes.name: SpeakerProfile
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a known speaker's resolution metadata: canonical key, role, GDELT stations, transcript venues, social handles, and known-flag.

nodes.name: resolve_speaker
nodes.summary: Resolves a raw speaker name to a SpeakerProfile by alias lookup and name-token matching, returning a generic profile when no known entry matches.

nodes.name: normalize_speaker_key
nodes.summary: Normalizes a speaker name into a lowercased underscore-joined token key used for registry and archive lookups.

nodes.name: whitehouse.py
nodes.summary: White House briefings client: scrapes the WH news listing for spoken-remarks articles and parses each into a speaker-attributed transcript record.
nodes.languageNotes: Regex-driven HTML scraping over truststore-backed aiohttp with asyncio.Semaphore-bounded article fetching; speaker inferred from URL slug.

nodes.name: extract_article_body
nodes.summary: Isolates the article content region of a White House page and strips scripts, tags, and entities to clean text.

nodes.name: build_record
nodes.summary: Builds a speaker-resolved transcript record from a White House article URL and HTML, skipping non-spoken-remarks pages.

nodes.name: extract_article_date
nodes.summary: Extracts the publication date of a White House article from page metadata, falling back to the date in the URL slug.

nodes.name: WhiteHouseClient
nodes.summary: Async client that paginates the White House news listing, filters to spoken-remarks articles, and fetches/builds transcript records concurrently.

nodes.name: premise_backtest.py
nodes.summary: Walk-forward backtest of the mentions corpus premise: scores per-speaker vs global mention predictions and evaluates skill with Brier scores, bootstrap intervals, and a paired sign test.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses numpy for vectorized squared-error/bootstrap math and scipy.stats.binomtest for the paired sign test; ScoredPrediction is a dataclass row type.

nodes.name: walk_forward_predictions_for_phrase
nodes.summary: Generates leak-free walk-forward predictions for a phrase, comparing a global base rate against a speaker-specific shrinkage estimate built only from prior-dated events.

nodes.name: bootstrap_skill_interval
nodes.summary: Computes a bootstrap confidence interval for the Brier skill score of speaker vs global predictions via resampled squared errors.

nodes.name: summarize_premise
nodes.summary: Aggregates a set of scored predictions into a summary of Brier scores, skill, bootstrap interval, sign-test significance, and sample counts.

nodes.name: paired_sign_test
nodes.summary: Runs a paired sign test on per-event squared errors to assess whether speaker predictions beat the global base rate at a statistically significant rate.

nodes.name: score_corpus_premise
nodes.summary: Pools walk-forward predictions across all phrases into a single corpus-wide prediction set for premise evaluation.

nodes.name: ScoredPrediction
nodes.summary: Dataclass row capturing one walk-forward prediction: phrase, speaker, date, realized outcome, and both global and speaker probability estimates.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed archive for the mentions signal: persists transcripts, discovery targets, cached GDELT base rates, and hearing schedules, with phrase counting, staleness tracking, and retention pruning.
nodes.languageNotes: Single class wrapping a sqlite3 connection with executemany upserts, dynamic WHERE-clause builders for phrase counting, TTL-based GDELT cache, and refresh-log staleness.

nodes.name: MentionsArchiveStore
nodes.summary: SQLite store for the mentions archive exposing transcript upsert/count, discovery-target and hearing-schedule persistence, a TTL'd GDELT base-rate cache, staleness checks, and retention pruning.
nodes.languageNotes: Class-level DEFAULT_DB_PATH / GDELT_TTL_SECONDS / DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS constants; phrase counts done in Python over normalized text rather than SQL LIKE.

nodes.name: make_doc_id
nodes.summary: Derives a stable SHA-256 document id from a transcript's source, URL, and speaker key for upsert deduplication.

nodes.name: refresh_mentions_archive.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that refreshes the mentions archive: discovers speaker targets from markets, pulls Fed/Congress/GovInfo/White House sources via their clients, upserts into the store, and prunes old data.
nodes.languageNotes: argparse CLI driving asyncio.run; per-source refreshers gated by store.is_stale TTLs; _Clients dataclass bundles the four external clients.

nodes.name: run_refresh
nodes.summary: Iterates the registered per-source refreshers, skipping any whose data is still fresh per TTL, marking each refreshed, then prunes the store of expired records.

nodes.name: discover_targets
nodes.summary: Scans mention markets to discover distinct speaker/venue targets by parsing titles and resolving speakers, deduplicating before persistence.

nodes.name: _run
nodes.summary: Async driver that wires up the store and all four external clients, discovers targets, runs the refresh, and closes every resource.

nodes.name: load_markets
nodes.summary: Loads a markets snapshot JSON file into a normalized list of market dicts, tolerating both bare-list and wrapped formats.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: argparse CLI entry point that parses staleness and markets-file flags and dispatches the async refresh run.

nodes.name: love_island_agent.py
nodes.summary: Defines the LoveIslandAgent that orchestrates YouTube teaser/transcript lookups and Grok-X sentiment searches, then turns Claude's qualitative read into a deterministic SignalEstimate for Love Island markets.
nodes.languageNotes: Async agent wrapping BaseAgent with tool callbacks (_search_youtube_teasers, _fetch_youtube_transcript, _search_x_sentiment) exposed to the LLM; loads its system prompt from love_island.md.

nodes.name: LoveIslandAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that scopes a Love Island market scan: searches YouTube teasers, fetches episode transcripts, runs Grok-X sentiment, gates mentions markets on transcript evidence, and emits a lineage-bearing SignalEstimate.

nodes.name: youtube_client.py
nodes.summary: Async YouTube Data API client plus transcript fetcher used to find Love Island teaser/clip videos and pull their spoken-text transcripts for the signal pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aiohttp for the Data API and runs the synchronous youtube-transcript-api in an executor via run_in_executor; truststore-backed SSL context for the corporate proxy.

nodes.name: parse_search_results
nodes.summary: Parses a YouTube search.list JSON response into normalized video records, skipping non-video items and guarding against malformed payloads.

nodes.name: parse_video_list
nodes.summary: Parses a YouTube videos.list JSON response into normalized video records keyed by video id.

nodes.name: YouTubeClient
nodes.summary: Async client exposing search_videos, list_videos, and fetch_transcript over the YouTube Data API and youtube-transcript-api, with lazy aiohttp session management and an async context-manager lifecycle.

nodes.name: love_island.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs the LoveIslandAgent for a given ticker and prints the resulting signal estimates as JSON; the locally runnable wrapper for the love-island signal.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments (ticker, channel, market context), runs the LoveIslandAgent asynchronously, and prints the produced signal estimates as JSON.

nodes.name: love_island.py
nodes.summary: Converter that maps the agent's probability and categorical evidence_strength to a deterministic SignalEstimate weight/uncertainty, and gates mentions markets behind transcript evidence so the LLM cannot inflate its own confidence.
nodes.languageNotes: Evidence tiers (teaser_confirmed/teaser_hinted/sentiment_only/prior_only) map to fixed weight/uncertainty, decoupling the model's qualitative probability from how heavily it is trusted downstream.

nodes.name: mentions_transcript_gate
nodes.summary: Returns an instruction string forcing the agent to fetch a transcript before building a mentions signal, or None when the gate is satisfied or the market is not a mentions market.

nodes.name: build_love_island_signal
nodes.summary: Builds a lineage-bearing SignalEstimate from a probability, evidence tier, and market bucket, validating the bucket and clamping derived values so the agent controls only the probability, never the trust weight.

nodes.name: love_island_lexicon.py
nodes.summary: Curated lexicon of Love Island franchise catchphrase base rates plus a lookup helper that returns a prior probability for a candidate phrase in mentions markets.

nodes.name: lookup_catchphrase_prior
nodes.summary: Looks up a normalized phrase against the curated catchphrase prior table, returning the matching base-rate probability for mentions-market scoring.

nodes.name: love_island_x_accounts.py
nodes.summary: Curated list of high-engagement Love Island X handles and hashtags plus a helper that builds a query suffix to focus Grok's x_search on the right fandom conversation.
nodes.languageNotes: Project does not use the X/Twitter API directly; this scopes Grok's x_search tool, mirroring how the weather agent restricts to named meteorologist authorities.

nodes.name: love_island_x_query_focus
nodes.summary: Builds a query suffix appended to Grok's x_search that prioritizes the curated Love Island handles and hashtags without hard-restricting the broader fandom sentiment.

nodes.name: db.py
nodes.summary: Supabase persistence layer for the trading system: async helpers that insert and upsert signals, trades, reviewed ideas, recommendations, positions, markets, and Polymarket rows, plus queries for fair values, open positions, and recommendation history.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of the Supabase Python client's fluent table().select()/insert()/upsert() chaining with async execute(); upserts are batched in fixed-size slices to avoid oversized requests.

nodes.name: ideas_history.py
nodes.summary: Pure-function helpers that parse ISO timestamps and join recommendation rows with their price marks into a sorted timeline, shared by both the Supabase and JSONL recommendation stores.

nodes.name: paper.py
nodes.summary: File-backed (JSONL) paper-trading ledger that records trade-idea recommendations, computes mark and settlement values in cents, appends periodic marks, and produces performance scorecards by source, disposition, and edge bucket.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses append-only JSONL files as a lightweight local store; cent-denominated P&L math keeps the paper ledger independent of the live database.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dashboard backend that defines the HTTP API (state, config, logs, ideas approve/reject, market prices) and runs background pollers that fetch Kalshi balance, positions, orders, and fills, reconciling closed positions from fills and the settled-positions API.
nodes.languageNotes: Route handlers are nested closures inside create_app() and registered via decorators; background pollers run as asyncio tasks started on the FastAPI startup event, gathering per-ticker price fetches under a concurrency semaphore.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: In-memory dashboard state model: dataclasses for log lines, agent statuses, and the aggregate TradingState that the server mutates via partial updates and serializes to JSON for the frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclasses with default_factory for mutable defaults; apply_update() coerces incoming partial dicts (timestamps, nested agent-status maps) before assignment.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and caches the async Supabase client from configured URL and service-role key, raising a RuntimeError when credentials are missing.

nodes.name: insert_signal
nodes.summary: Inserts a single signal row (probability, ticker, optional trade linkage, issued-at timestamp) into the signals table.

nodes.name: resolve_market
nodes.summary: Marks all unresolved signal rows for a ticker with the realized YES outcome and back-fills each row's Brier score from its stored probability.

nodes.name: insert_trade
nodes.summary: Persists an executed trade row combining the trade idea, order result, risk decision, and contract count into the trades table.

nodes.name: insert_reviewed_idea
nodes.summary: Writes a reviewed (approved or rejected) trade idea with its risk decision and review timestamp to the reviewed_ideas table.

nodes.name: insert_recommendation
nodes.summary: Upserts a recommendation row (predicted probability, edge, sizing, disposition, sources) keyed on id into the recommendations table.

nodes.name: get_fair_values_from_recommendations
nodes.summary: Returns the most recent predicted probability (fair value) per ticker by querying recommendations ordered by cycle timestamp.

nodes.name: get_latest_fair_values
nodes.summary: Returns the latest combined probability per ticker from the scored_markets table ordered by creation time.

nodes.name: close_trade
nodes.summary: Closes a trade row by recording its exit reason, realized P&L in dollars, and a UTC closed-at timestamp.

nodes.name: open_position
nodes.summary: Inserts a new open position row linking a trade idea, fill result, opening trade id, and contract count with a normalized opened-at timestamp.

nodes.name: close_position
nodes.summary: Closes the open position for a ticker, guarding against concurrent closure, stamps realized P&L and the closing trade id, and cascades to close the linked trade row.

nodes.name: _prepare_polymarket_row
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw Polymarket market dict into a database row, parsing JSON-encoded outcome/price arrays, casting numeric and boolean fields, and deriving the YES price.

nodes.name: upsert_polymarket_markets
nodes.summary: Prepares and batch-upserts Polymarket market rows keyed on condition_id, logging and tolerating partial batch failures.

nodes.name: prepare_market_row
nodes.summary: Builds a Kalshi markets table row from a raw market dict, coercing prices, volume, open interest, and close timestamp via the typed helpers.

nodes.name: upsert_markets
nodes.summary: Batch-upserts Kalshi market rows keyed on ticker, splitting into fixed-size batches and counting successfully written rows.

nodes.name: recommendations_with_marks
nodes.summary: Fetches all recommendations and recommendation marks, normalizes their rows, and joins them into per-recommendation timelines via the shared ideas-history helper.

nodes.name: fetch_open_recommendations
nodes.summary: Returns open recommendations newer than a max-age window, parsing each row's cycle timestamp via the shared ISO parser to filter by recency.

nodes.name: parse_iso_timestamp
nodes.summary: Parses an ISO-8601 string (tolerating a trailing Z) into a datetime, returning None on failure.

nodes.name: join_recommendations_and_marks
nodes.summary: Groups price marks by recommendation id and builds a per-recommendation object with a chronologically sorted mark timeline, sorting the overall result by cycle timestamp.

nodes.name: mark_value_cents
nodes.summary: Computes the current mark value in cents for a YES or NO side from the live YES bid/ask quote.

nodes.name: compute_mark
nodes.summary: Computes an unrealized or realized mark for a paper position, returning settlement P&L when the market has resolved and live mark-to-market P&L otherwise.

nodes.name: _read_jsonl
nodes.summary: Reads a JSONL file into a list of dict rows, skipping blank lines and returning an empty list when the file is absent.

nodes.name: record_recommendation
nodes.summary: Appends a new paper-trading recommendation row (ticker, side, entry price, predicted probability, edge, sources, sizing, disposition, reasoning) with a generated UUID to the recommendations JSONL.

nodes.name: open_recommendations
nodes.summary: Loads recommendations and returns those still open within a max-age window, using the shared ISO parser to filter by cycle timestamp recency.

nodes.name: recommendations_with_marks
nodes.summary: Reads recommendation and mark JSONL files and joins them into per-recommendation timelines via the shared ideas-history helper.

nodes.name: performance_by_source
nodes.summary: Aggregates the latest marks by each recommendation's first source and returns a per-source scorecard of paper-trading performance.

nodes.name: performance_by_disposition
nodes.summary: Aggregates the latest marks by recommendation disposition and returns a per-disposition performance scorecard.

nodes.name: performance_by_edge_bucket
nodes.summary: Buckets recommendations by their predicted edge in cents and returns a per-bucket performance scorecard.

nodes.name: compute_closed_positions
nodes.summary: Reconstructs closed positions from cached fills by pairing buy and sell fills per ticker, computing average-cost entry/exit prices and realized P&L in dollars.

nodes.name: merge_with_settled_positions
nodes.summary: Augments fills-derived closed positions with entries from the Kalshi settled-positions API that the fills feed missed, deriving entry price from cached buy fills.

nodes.name: _fetch_yes_price_data
nodes.summary: Fetches a market's YES bid/ask and last price from the Kalshi API under a concurrency semaphore, returning a normalized price dict.

nodes.name: _fetch_yes_price_cents
nodes.summary: Fetches a single market's YES price in cents from the Kalshi API under a concurrency semaphore.

nodes.name: _poll_kalshi_account
nodes.summary: Background poller that periodically fetches Kalshi balance, positions, and orders, enriches them with live YES prices and fair values, and writes the result into the shared trading state.

nodes.name: _poll_fills
nodes.summary: Background poller that fetches Kalshi fills, caches them per ticker, reconstructs closed positions, and merges in settled-positions API data, updating the trading state.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Factory that builds the FastAPI app, wires the trading state and config manager, registers all HTTP routes (state, config, logs, ideas approve/reject, market prices), and launches the account and fills pollers on startup.

nodes.name: LogLine
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single dashboard log entry with timestamp, message, and severity level.

nodes.name: AgentStatus
nodes.summary: Dataclass tracking a signal agent's enabled flag, status, last run time, last signal count, and last output summary.

nodes.name: TradingState
nodes.summary: Central in-memory dashboard state holding balance, P&L, positions, orders, ideas, agent statuses, and the event log; supports appending log lines, applying partial updates with coercion, and serializing to a JSON-ready dict.

nodes.name: kalshi_auth.py
nodes.summary: Low-level synchronous Kalshi API client that handles RSA-PSS request signing, environment-based credential loading, and authenticated GET requests against the Kalshi REST API.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the cryptography library's serialization and RSA-PSS padding to sign request payloads; a lightweight hand-rolled .env loader avoids a python-dotenv dependency.

nodes.name: _load_dotenv
nodes.summary: Minimal .env file parser that reads KEY=VALUE lines and populates os.environ via setdefault without overriding existing variables.

nodes.name: KalshiClient
nodes.summary: Synchronous Kalshi REST client that loads an RSA private key, signs each request with a timestamp+method+path payload, and issues authenticated GET calls over a pooled requests Session.
nodes.languageNotes: from_env is a classmethod factory; _sign builds the KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE header with RSA-PSS over SHA256.

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: Asynchronous Kalshi API client wrapping the synchronous signing client in an executor, exposing high-level methods for series, events, markets, orderbooks, balances, positions, fills, orders, candlesticks, and trades with response normalization.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges blocking requests-based I/O into asyncio via run_in_executor and asyncio.to_thread; supports async context-manager protocol (__aenter__/__aexit__).

nodes.name: KalshiClient
nodes.summary: Async facade over the synchronous signing client offering domain-specific Kalshi endpoint helpers, normalizing markets and orderbooks on read and guarding order placement behind a write-permission check with retry logic.
nodes.languageNotes: create_order/cancel_order route through with_retry for resilience and raise PermissionError when writes are disabled; read methods delegate to normalize_market and normalize_orderbook.

nodes.name: read_only_client.py
nodes.summary: Read-only proxy around the async KalshiClient that whitelists safe read methods and raises on any write or unknown attribute access, used to expose market data to the dashboard without trade-execution risk.
nodes.languageNotes: Overrides __getattr__/__setattr__ to block mutation; uses object.__setattr__ in __init__ to seed the wrapped client past the locked-down setter.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyKalshiClient
nodes.summary: Wraps a KalshiClient and exposes only explicitly listed read endpoints, denying access to write operations and undefined attributes to enforce a read-only dashboard surface.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Normalization helpers that coerce raw Kalshi API payloads into consistent cent-denominated and float fields for markets and orderbook levels.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses module-level mapping tables (_DOLLAR_TO_CENTS, _FP_TO_NUMERIC) to declaratively drive field conversions.

nodes.name: normalize_market
nodes.summary: Returns a copy of a raw market dict with dollar-denominated fields converted to integer cents and floating-point fields coerced to numeric values.

nodes.name: normalize_orderbook
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw orderbook response into structured yes/no price levels with cent prices and numeric quantities.

nodes.name: _normalize_levels
nodes.summary: Converts a list of raw orderbook level tuples into normalized records with cent prices and integer counts.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project coding-convention guide for the Kalshi Market Scorer, mandating fully spelled-out variable names (with an extensive bad-to-good mapping) and a safe convention for formatting and linking Kalshi market tickers.

nodes.name: KALSHI_SETUP.md
nodes.summary: Team setup guide for Kalshi API credentials, covering per-developer API keys on a shared account, gitignored secrets placement, .env configuration, auth testing, secure key-sharing options, and remediation for a previously leaked key.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency pin list for the project, declaring runtime libraries (aiohttp, pydantic, anthropic, fastapi, uvicorn, supabase, scipy) and the test toolchain (pytest with asyncio and mock plugins).

nodes.name: runtime_config.json
nodes.summary: Central runtime tuning configuration for the scoring pipeline, defining signal-source weights, per-signal uncertainty parameters, microstructure/order-flow thresholds, Kalshi-bias and exit/filter rules, model selection, and per-agent enable flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Flat single-level JSON object of scalar tuning knobs, several prefixed by domain (weight_*, uncertainty_*, mm_*, poly_*, bias_*, exit_*, filter_*, agent_*_enabled) for grouping by concern.

nodes.name: data_orchestrator.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the trade-idea orchestrator agent that synthesizes specialist SignalEstimates, runs a four-question adversarial challenge (bear case, source independence, base rate, fresh-eyes), applies orthogonal-source weighting rules, and emits a ranked JSON slate of surviving trade ideas via build_trade_idea.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes a deterministic decision procedure (signal weighting + four-gate challenge) directly in natural-language prompt form, with a fenced JSON output contract.

nodes.name: kalshi_bias.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the calibration specialist agent that applies known Kalshi pricing-bias corrections (longshot overpricing, political underconfidence, near-certainty compression) to a market price and returns a corrected probability SignalEstimate, or [] when the delta is under 3 cents.

nodes.name: love_island.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the Love Island signal specialist that estimates YES probability for reality-TV markets from same-day YouTube teasers and X fan sentiment, bucketing markets (binary_event, elimination, winner, mentions), enforcing strict same-day-teaser and no-fabrication rules, and emitting a SignalEstimate with an evidence_strength tier.
nodes.languageNotes: Notable anti-fabrication guardrails: absence of evidence must return [] rather than a low probability, and the mentions bucket requires an actual transcript before a signal can be built.

nodes.name: market_maker.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the market-microstructure specialist that detects market-maker liquidity withdrawal via spread widening (>8 cents) and order-book depth imbalance, returning a directional probability SignalEstimate when an anomaly is found or [] otherwise.

nodes.name: order_flow.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the order-flow specialist that detects informed-trader accumulation using VPIN and Order Flow Imbalance, gating on minimum recent-trade activity and threshold scores before emitting a directional probability SignalEstimate.

nodes.name: polymarket_price.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the cross-platform price specialist that matches a Kalshi market to its Polymarket counterpart, verifies CLOB order-book depth, checks the price gap against thresholds, and emits a SignalEstimate from the cross-venue price discrepancy or [] with a stated reason.

nodes.name: polymarket_whale.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the whale-activity monitor that checks whether top-PnL Polymarket leaderboard wallets are positioned on the matched market, emitting a smart-money positioning SignalEstimate independent of the price gap or [] when no tracked wallets are present.

nodes.name: weather.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the weather specialist that derives a market probability primarily from the 31-member GEFS ensemble empirical CDF (with a NOAA parametric fallback), adds an independent X meteorologist-authority estimate, applies same-day observed-extreme overrides at the settlement station, and emits one or two SignalEstimates.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes a multi-source forecasting procedure where the ensemble CDF sets the probability, NWS gridpoint/AFD are context-only, and a same-day live-observation override conditions the empirical CDF on realized extremes.

nodes.name: x.md
nodes.summary: System prompt for the X (Twitter) social-signal specialist that runs category-default search strategies, escalates to expert/news strategies on high uncertainty, synthesizes a qualitative sentiment direction from actual post content rather than a probability threshold, and emits SignalEstimates via build_x_signal.

nodes.name: series_contract_terms.json
nodes.summary: Cached lookup of Kalshi contract settlement terms keyed by series ticker, mapping each of 141 series to its settlement data sources (name/URL), official contract-terms PDF links, and a fetched-at timestamp. Used to ground market scoring and adversarial challenge in authoritative resolution criteria.
nodes.languageNotes: Large flat JSON dictionary keyed by uppercase series ticker; each value carries settlement_sources, contract_terms_url, contract_url, and an ISO-8601 fetched_at timestamp, indicating a periodically refreshed cache rather than hand-maintained config.

nodes.name: series_settlement_stations.json
nodes.summary: Maps weather-market series tickers (high/low temperature and rain series for 41 cities) to their NWS settlement weather station, including station_id, latitude, longitude, source_type, and resolution timestamp. Consumed by the weather signal pipeline to forecast at the exact settlement station rather than a city centroid.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON dictionary keyed by KXHIGH*/KXLOW*/KXRAIN* series tickers; each entry pins a station_id with lat/lon coordinates and source_type='nws_station', supporting station-accurate weather forecasting.

nodes.name: series_slugs.json
nodes.summary: Lookup table mapping lowercase Kalshi series tickers to their human-readable kalshi.com URL slugs, which cannot be derived from the ticker alone. Used to build canonical market deep links for the dashboard and reports.
nodes.languageNotes: Flat string-to-string JSON map (ticker -> slug); fills the gap that the Kalshi API does not expose the human-readable slug segment of a market URL.

nodes.name: 001_pipeline_schema.sql
nodes.summary: Full Supabase/Postgres pipeline schema for the Kalshi trader, defining eight idempotent tables (recommendations, marks, cycles, scored markets, market catalog, whale intelligence, whale trades, sportsbook odds) with their indexes. Additive and re-runnable; the pipeline falls back to local JSONL when these tables are absent.
nodes.languageNotes: All tables use `create table if not exists` with uuid primary keys defaulting to gen_random_uuid() and timestamptz audit columns; CHECK constraints enforce enum-like values (side, status) and unique indexes provide re-runnable dedupe keys.

nodes.name: recommendations
nodes.summary: Paper-trade calibration table recording each trade recommendation (ticker, side, entry price, predicted probability, edge, sources, suggested Kelly size) with an open/resolved lifecycle status and a paper_only flag.

nodes.name: recommendation_marks
nodes.summary: Mark-to-market history for recommendations, capturing current value, running PnL, would-profit flag, and resolution state via a foreign key to the recommendations table.

nodes.name: cycles
nodes.summary: Per-cycle pipeline run metadata tracking counts of markets scored, 2+ source markets, candidates, and approved ideas, keyed by a unique cycle timestamp.

nodes.name: scored_markets
nodes.summary: Per-cycle snapshot of every scored market with its bid/ask, volume, open interest, composite score, coverage, magnitude signals, directional signal estimates, combined probability, and edge — enabling signal backtesting and price/signal evolution tracking.

nodes.name: markets
nodes.summary: Kalshi market catalog (optional server-side migration of live_markets.json) keyed by ticker, storing core quote fields plus the raw row in jsonb so the scout can read the tradeable universe without parsing the 281MB snapshot.

nodes.name: whales
nodes.summary: Polymarket whale intelligence table from on-chain analysis, storing per-wallet realized/total PnL, volume, trade count, win rate, and provenance for the whale-following signal.

nodes.name: whale_trades
nodes.summary: Individual Polymarket whale trade records (wallet, condition, outcome, side, size, price, tx hash) with a multi-column unique dedupe index supporting re-runnable on-chain backfills even when tx_hash is null.

nodes.name: sportsbook_odds
nodes.summary: Sportsbook odds cache for the sports-market signal, storing implied/American/decimal odds per book and event with an optional kalshi_ticker linkage to compare bookmaker pricing against Kalshi markets.

nodes.name: 002_recommendation_disposition.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a `disposition` column (default 'candidate') plus its index to the recommendations table, so the pipeline can record and backtest every scored 2+ source market — not just the risk-approved slate — to check edge-bar calibration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `add column if not exists` so the migration is idempotent; disposition is orthogonal to the open/resolved status lifecycle.

nodes.name: 003_add_reasoning_to_recommendations.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a `reasoning` text column to the recommendations table to store the orchestrator's adversarial-challenge text for LLM-approved ideas (deterministically-scored ideas have no reasoning).
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotent `add column if not exists` with an empty-string default.

nodes.name: EXAMPLE_SCORE.md
nodes.summary: Worked example of a scoring run showing the top 20 actionable Kalshi markets, with run metadata, a signal legend, and guidance on interpreting the actionability report.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Operator guide for the market-scoring pipeline covering how to fetch live markets and run scoring, the architecture, the signal sources, the composite score formula, and the default scored categories.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes kalshi_trader.agents an importable Python package for the project's signal and challenge agents.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Static single-page portfolio-monitor dashboard ('KALSHI // PORTFOLIO MONITOR') rendering open positions and trade ideas, with inline styling and client-side JavaScript.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained HTML page bundling its CSS and JavaScript inline rather than referencing external assets.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes kalshi_trader.external an importable Python package for external-data-source client integrations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the mentions-signal module, documenting it as a speaker-attributed transcript corpus plus GDELT mention-rate cache.

nodes.name: price_study.py
nodes.summary: Pure numerical helpers for the Q6 mentions mispricing study: as-of price selection, Brier scoring, reliability and favorite-longshot calibration tables, and a hand-rolled logistic regression with bootstrap intervals and a likelihood-ratio test.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements logistic regression, bootstrap confidence intervals, and a likelihood-ratio test directly on numpy/scipy primitives, keeping the math dependency-light and unit-testable.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes kalshi_trader.pipelines an importable Python package for the project's signal and scoring pipelines.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes kalshi_trader.signals an importable Python package for the project's edge-signal implementations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes kalshi_trader.ui an importable Python package for the FastAPI dashboard server and templates.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Main Kalshi Trading Dashboard template served by the FastAPI UI server, rendering scored trade ideas with inline styling and client-side JavaScript for interaction and refresh.
nodes.languageNotes: Large single-page template (~1.5k lines) bundling CSS and JavaScript inline; served by kalshi_trader.ui.server.

nodes.name: select_asof_price
nodes.summary: Selects the market's price as of a target number of minutes before close from a candle series, falling back across mid/open/close fields and clamping to a valid probability range.

nodes.name: brier_score
nodes.summary: Computes the mean Brier score over a set of rows comparing a chosen prediction column against realized binary outcomes.

nodes.name: _bin_table
nodes.summary: Buckets rows into probability bins and computes per-bin counts, mean predicted price, and mean realized outcome — the shared engine behind the reliability and favorite-longshot tables.

nodes.name: fit_logistic_regression
nodes.summary: Fits a ridge-regularized logistic regression via Newton-Raphson iterations on standardized features, returning the fitted coefficient vector.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled Newton-Raphson solver using numpy.linalg.solve with a ridge term, avoiding a scikit-learn dependency.

nodes.name: logistic_predicted_probabilities
nodes.summary: Applies fitted logistic-regression coefficients to a feature matrix to produce predicted probabilities via the sigmoid function.

nodes.name: logistic_log_likelihood
nodes.summary: Computes the log-likelihood of binary outcomes under a logistic model's predicted probabilities, used to drive the likelihood-ratio test.

nodes.name: bootstrap_coefficient_intervals
nodes.summary: Estimates confidence intervals for logistic-regression coefficients by resampling rows with replacement, refitting on each bootstrap draw, and taking percentiles of the collected coefficient distribution.

nodes.name: likelihood_ratio_test
nodes.summary: Runs a chi-squared likelihood-ratio test comparing a full and reduced logistic model to assess whether added features (e.g. the GDELT rate) explain outcomes beyond the price alone.

nodes.name: run_ui.py
nodes.summary: Command-line launcher for the Kalshi Trader FastAPI dashboard that parses host/port options, reports the local network URL, and starts the kalshi_trader.ui.server app under uvicorn.

nodes.name: get_local_ip
nodes.summary: Determines the host's local network IP address by opening a throwaway UDP socket, falling back to 'localhost' on failure, so the launcher can print a LAN-accessible dashboard URL.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses --host/--port arguments, prints the local and network dashboard URLs, and launches the FastAPI app via uvicorn.run.

nodes.name: _avco_price
nodes.summary: Computes the count-weighted average price (average cost) over a list of fills, using the NO-side price (100 - yes_price) when use_no_price is set, otherwise the yes_price; returns 0.0 for empty fill lists.

layers.name: Domain Models & Configuration

layers.name: Data Access Clients
layers.description: Async/sync clients for every data source the system reads: the authenticated Kalshi REST and WebSocket API, plus external feeds (Polymarket, ESPN, NOAA/Open-Meteo, FiveThirtyEight, GDELT, x.ai Grok, YouTube, Fed/GovInfo/Congress/White House) and their per-source parsers and registries.

layers.name: Deterministic Signal Builders

layers.name: Scoring & Risk Engine

layers.name: Mentions Archive Subsystem
layers.description: The SQLite-backed mentions corpus: transcript/base-rate store, walk-forward premise backtest, and price-study analytics for the GDELT mentions signal.

layers.name: Pipelines & CLI Entry Points

layers.name: Persistence & Data Schema
layers.description: State storage layers: the Supabase async persistence helpers, JSONL paper-trading ledger, recommendation-history joins, market snapshot serialization, and the Postgres pipeline schema with its migrations and tables.

layers.name: Dashboard & UI
layers.description: Two FastAPI surfaces — the read-only ideas dashboard (app factory, routes, background scoring loop, read-only client guard) and the operator UI (server, in-memory trading state, tunable config manager) — with their HTML templates.

layers.name: Project Documentation & Setup
layers.description: Top-level project guidance and tooling config: coding-convention guide, Kalshi credential setup, the dependency pin list, and the analysis ignore file.

tour.title: Project Orientation
tour.description: There is no README at the repository root, so the authoritative orientation lives in two project guides. CLAUDE.md lays out the non-negotiable coding conventions for the Kalshi Market Scorer (spell every variable name out in full, and always wrap a market ticker in a safe series link), while KALSHI_SETUP.md walks through wiring per-developer Kalshi API credentials via a gitignored key and a .env file. requirements.txt rounds out the picture, revealing the stack at a glance: aiohttp for async I/O, anthropic for the LLM agents, fastapi/uvicorn for the dashboards, supabase for persistence, and scipy for the statistics. Read these first to understand the rules of the road before touching code.

tour.title: The Domain Vocabulary
tour.description: models.py is the single most depended-upon file in the entire codebase (37 modules import it), so it is the right place to learn the system's vocabulary. It defines the shared dataclasses and enums that every layer speaks in: a Market is a tradeable Kalshi contract, a SignalEstimate is one source's probability opinion, a ScoredMarket fuses signals into an edge, a TradeIdea is a risk-approved recommendation, and a Position is something you currently hold. Internalize these types now; every later step manipulates them.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses (@dataclass) generate __init__, __repr__, and __eq__ from typed field declarations, giving you lightweight, self-documenting value objects without boilerplate. Paired with Enum, they make illegal states unrepresentable across module boundaries.

tour.title: Configuration & Tunable Parameters
tour.description: With the vocabulary in hand, see how the system is configured. config.py (fan-in 26) loads the .env file into the environment and exposes API keys, endpoints, and trading parameters as importable constants. runtime_config.json is the hot-tunable knob board — signal-source weights, per-signal uncertainty, microstructure thresholds, bias and exit rules, model selection, and per-agent enable flags — and config_manager.py (fan-in 12) is the typed store that loads, validates, persists, and serves those values to every signal and agent at runtime. Together they let operators retune the scorer without editing code.

tour.title: The Kalshi API Clients
tour.description: Every quote, balance, and order flows through the Kalshi API layer. kalshi_auth.py is the low-level synchronous client that handles RSA-PSS request signing and credential loading (this is what the KALSHI_SETUP.md from Step 1 configures), and client.py wraps it in an executor to expose async high-level methods for series, markets, orderbooks, positions, fills, and candlesticks. read_only_client.py is the crucial safety layer: a proxy that whitelists only read methods and raises on any write, so the dashboards can surface live market data with zero trade-execution risk. This read-only guard is how the project keeps prod safe by default.
tour.languageLesson: Wrapping a blocking synchronous client with loop.run_in_executor is the standard Python pattern for exposing legacy sync code (here, RSA signing + HTTP) to an asyncio program without blocking the event loop.

tour.title: Market Discovery & Actionability Scoring
tour.description: Before any signal runs, the system has to find markets worth scoring. scanner.py fetches open Kalshi markets, enriches categories, filters by category and expiry, refreshes live prices in concurrency-bounded batches, and drives scoring with order-flow and orderbook enrichment. The actionability subsystem ranks the candidates: scorer.py combines weighted volume, open interest, momentum, and microstructure into a single composite score, while signals.py is the library of pure functions that normalize each raw input (spread, settlement proximity, volume, order flow) into a sub-score. The actionability README documents the composite-score formula and default categories. This is the funnel that narrows thousands of contracts down to a tradeable shortlist.
tour.languageLesson: scanner.py uses an asyncio.Semaphore to bound concurrent API calls (a fan-out pattern): many price refreshes are launched as coroutines but only N run at once, balancing throughput against Kalshi rate limits.

tour.title: External Data Source Clients
tour.description: The edge comes from signals, and signals come from outside data. This layer holds an async client per source: polymarket.py matches Kalshi markets to their Polymarket equivalents and detects whale entries, noaa.py pulls NWS gridpoint forecasts and observed temperature extremes for weather markets, and x_client.py talks to x.ai's Grok Agent Tools API for live X search and weather-authority forecasts. Each client speaks one vendor's protocol and returns raw data; none of them decides what a market is worth yet. Notice the breadth here — this is what makes the scorer multi-source rather than a single-model bet.

tour.title: LLM Signal Agents
tour.description: On top of the raw clients sit the Claude-driven specialist agents. base.py wraps the AsyncAnthropic client and drives the multi-turn tool-use conversation loop that every agent inherits (fan-in 9). weather_agent.py is the richest specialist (fan-out 13): it fuses NOAA forecasts, GEFS ensembles, and independent meteorologist authorities into weather probability signals, guided entirely by its system prompt weather.md. parsing.py (fan-in 20) is the shared glue that turns each agent's LLM output back into the SignalEstimate dataclass from Step 2. Read the prompt alongside the agent — in this system the markdown prompt is as much 'source code' as the Python is.
tour.languageLesson: The tool-use loop in base.py is the agentic pattern: the model is given tool schemas, it requests a tool call, the Python runtime executes it and feeds the result back, and the loop repeats until the model emits a final answer (here, a structured SignalEstimate).

tour.title: Deterministic Signal Builders
tour.description: Not every signal needs an LLM. The signal-builder layer holds pure functions that convert raw source data straight into SignalEstimate objects. weather.py turns NWS forecasts and GEFS ensemble CDFs into probability estimates (the deterministic backbone the weather agent from Step 7 reasons over), while microstructure.py folds momentum, order-flow imbalance, orderbook skew, and range position into a directional estimate with no model call at all. Keeping the math deterministic and testable here means the LLM agents can be reserved for genuinely qualitative judgment, and every probability remains reproducible.

tour.title: Synthesis & Adversarial Challenge
tour.description: Once the specialists have spoken, their estimates must be reconciled into ranked trade ideas. data_orchestrator_agent.py aggregates every per-market SignalEstimate, presents them to Claude, and parses the result into TradeIdea models — but the real intelligence is in its prompt, data_orchestrator.md (fan-out 8). That prompt runs a four-question adversarial challenge (state the bear case, verify source independence, check the base rate, take a fresh-eyes look) and applies orthogonal-source weighting before emitting a slate. consensus.py adds a second guardrail, requiring agreement across a minimum number of agents before any ticker/side is accepted. This is where raw signals become defensible recommendations.

tour.title: Risk Sizing, Execution & Exits
tour.description: A surviving idea still has to be sized, placed, and watched. risk.py vets each idea against exposure, time-to-close, and edge thresholds, then sizes the position via half-Kelly with loss-streak damping and estimates Kalshi trading fees. executor.py translates a risk-approved TradeIdea into a concrete order (contract count and limit price) and can bulk-cancel resting orders, while exit_monitor.py watches open entries against Polymarket prices to flag profit-taking, volume-spike, and stale-thesis exits. This is the engine that connects an opinion to actual capital — and the prod gating from Step 1 lives at this boundary.
tour.languageLesson: Half-Kelly sizing bets half of the full-Kelly-optimal fraction (edge divided by odds). It sacrifices some long-run growth for a large reduction in variance and drawdown — the standard practical compromise when edge estimates are themselves uncertain, as they are here.

tour.title: Pipelines: Wiring One Signal End-to-End
tour.description: Each signal also has a standalone command-line entry point under pipelines/, which is the cleanest way to see a single signal wired from input to output. pipelines/weather.py runs the WeatherAgent for one ticker and prints its SignalEstimates as JSON; pipelines/mentions.py runs the GDELT-backed mentions signal end-to-end (parse the market title, fetch a base rate, emit an estimate). The orchestrator invokes exactly these CLIs to gather signals per market, so reading one pipeline top-to-bottom ties together the clients (Step 6), agents (Step 7), and builders (Step 8) you have already met.

tour.title: Persistence & the Database Schema
tour.description: Recommendations are only useful if you can score yourself later, so the system records everything it does. db.py is the async Supabase persistence layer (insert/upsert signals, trades, recommendations, positions, and Polymarket rows), with paper.py providing a file-backed JSONL fallback ledger and ideas_history.py joining recommendation rows to their price marks into a timeline. The Postgres schema in 001_pipeline_schema.sql defines eight idempotent tables; the recommendations table (fan-in 3) captures each idea's entry price, predicted probability, edge, and Kelly size, while scored_markets snapshots every market per cycle to enable signal backtesting. Note the design choice: the pipeline degrades gracefully to local JSONL when these tables are absent.
tour.languageLesson: These SQL migrations are written to be additive and re-runnable (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, idempotent indexes). Idempotent migrations can be applied repeatedly without error, which is what lets the same schema file safely bootstrap a fresh database or top up an existing one.

tour.title: The Operator UI & Read-Only Dashboard
tour.description: Finally, the surfaces a human actually looks at. run_ui.py is the detected process entry point — a uvicorn launcher — and ui/server.py is the operator backend that exposes the HTTP API (state, config, approve/reject ideas, market prices) and runs background pollers reconciling balance, positions, and fills, rendered through ui/templates/index.html. Separately, the read-only ideas dashboard (dashboard/app.py, its routes, and a background scoring_loop.py) re-runs the scanner on an interval and serves grouped ideas through the read-only client from Step 4 — no write path, no execution risk. grouping.py is the shared serializer that turns scored markets into the event-grouped payloads both surfaces render. You have now traced a market from discovery all the way to the screen where an operator approves the trade.
